Name: Monzon-1
Notes: First dedicated H2 well in EU

Name: Le Grand-Fougeray
Geological Setting: Low-grade metamorphosed black shales of Ordovician Traveusot Formation, Armorican Massif
Notes: Grey monazite nodules poor in thorium and rich in europium

Name: Fen Complex
Notes: Monazite occurs as a secondary REE mineral in hydrothermally altered rødbergite
Source: Rare Earths Norway, JORC resource (2024); Marien, C. (2019) PhD Thesis

Name: Serrabal
Notes: Crucial source for European silicon metal and polysilicon production
Source: Ferroglobe PLC Form 20-F; Ed Conway (2024) Material World

Name: Drag (Tysfjord)
Status: Exploited by The Quartz Corp; processes both local and imported high-purity quartz

Name: Nasafjell
Size / Reserves: Europe's largest hydrothermal vein quartz deposit
Notes: Evaluated by Elkem for silicon metal industry

Name: Švedlár
Geological Setting: Gemerid unit of the Western Carpathians
Source: Lens length 140 m, up to 40 m thick; inactive since 1990

Name: Vysoký Kámen
Notes: Primary raw material for European ceramics and glass industry
Source: Czech Geological Survey / Jirásek et al. (2020)

Name: Evje-Iveland
Size / Reserves: Regional field with hundreds of pegmatites
Notes: K-feldspar shows extreme fractionation and is enriched in trace elements
Source: Müller et al. (2015); Geological Survey of Norway (NGU)

Name: Barroso-Alvão
Grade / Concentration: K-feldspar acts as a host and indicator of Li enrichment
Notes: Barren to Li-rich pegmatites with blocky perthitic K-feldspar crystals
Source: Garate-Olave et al., Can. J. Min. Pet. (2025)

Name: Caldas do Gerês
Notes: High iron content; located in a National Park with strict environmental restrictions [cite: 52, 53]

Name: Beira Alta (Viseu, Sátão, Mangualde, Nelas)
Notes: Best balance of purity and low iron; close to major transport routes [cite: 63, 72]

Name: Guarda / Seia
Notes: Requires more intensive processing to remove iron [cite: 64]

Name: Norte de Portugal (Minho e Trás-os-Montes)
Notes: Quality is variable due to accessory minerals [cite: 65]

Name: Centro Litoral (Coimbra e Leiria)
Host Rock / Reservoir: Feldspathic sands mixed with quartz and clays [cite: 66]
Notes: Requires intensive separation from quartz and clays [cite: 67]

Name: Alentejo
Notes: Extracted as a byproduct of lithium mining [cite: 69]
